body:
## Purpose 
Allow for the user to pick which retention curve and hydraulic conductivity curve formulation they wish to use. These are paired - a single formulation determines both. We can support van Genuchten and Brooks and Corey to start.

A future PR will make the derivative of dpsidtheta continuous across S = 1 [which will help with timestepping the variability saturated case].

## To-do
- [X] Test BC on some runs, including freeze thaw and evaporation. They ran! it looked OK. - 
- [X] Updated design docs. The only modification required is to psi(S) for S>1, to ensure continuity. for BC, psi(S=1) is not zero, but psi_b.

## Content
Creates parameter struct which identifies the model formulation.
Switches to using this/unpacking this/dispatching off of this instead of being hardcoded for van Genuchten

Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

----
- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: allow for different hydrology closure models
